ce-workflow-dqh.description: Brainstorm workspace created by /work-brainstorm for: look at this repo, clone it to temp and analyze + the description. the idea here is for we to have a stable test setup where we can evaluate our changes - for performance, accuracy and token usage. different modes, different agents we use (like own reviewer vs ce-code review agents, different thinking levels, etc).that system should be both simple enough to be fast to test ce-workflow and complex enough to actually test the full suite. the goal is to further optimize ce-workflow while ensuring it is still working great

look at this repo, clone it to temp and analyze + the description. the idea here is for we to have a stable test setup where we can evaluate our changes - for performance, accuracy and token usage. different modes, different agents we use (like own reviewer vs ce-code review agents, different thinking levels, etc).that system should be both simple enough to be fast to test ce-workflow and complex enough to actually test the full suite. the goal is to further optimize ce-workflow while ensuring it is still working great
legacy.notes:
wo:idea schema=1 status=discussed
Brainstorm critic gate (bead-advisor) reviewed `docs/plans/2026-07-15-001-feat-ce-workflow-evaluation-harness-plan.md` and returned CONCERNS. Concrete findings: define positive cost thresholds and missing-metric outcomes; require every baseline/candidate sample to pass hard gates; define paired-run order/isolation/retry rules; version rubric anchors, critical dimensions, ties, and evaluator failures; fingerprint all non-factor settings and reject undeclared/no-op deltas; make project contracts and golden approvals testable and non-leaking; make full-pipeline sentinel triggers mandatory; require explicit per-depth time/token budgets. Artifact revised to address all eight findings in R1-R31 and AE1-AE10. First advisor output: `C:/tmp/compound-engineering/ce-brainstorm/workflow-eval-e3pzr1/advisor.md`.
